title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Martin Short stars in 'Marty, Life is Short,' a new documentary streaming on Netflix.
'Mars Attacks!' (1996) is now available for free on YouTube and Plex.
'Pop Culture Jeopardy!' Season 2, hosted by Colin Jost, is now streaming on Netflix.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu proposes a $4.9 billion budget with minimal spending increases amid financial challenges.
Several Boston City Council members consider rejecting Wu's budget to push for increased spending and fewer cuts.
Council must decide on the budget by June 10; failure to act means Wu's budget takes effect automatically.

title: Scholars bar is closing after 15 years. A new concept is already planned.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Scholars American Bistro & Cocktail Lounge will close after 15 years in Downtown Crossing on Saturday.
The bar will be replaced by Off The Rails Boston, focusing on live music and Americana-inspired cuisine.
The change reflects adaptations in Boston's downtown nightlife due to shifts in work and commuter patterns.

title: How much are you spending to attend the World Cup?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston will host seven FIFA World Cup 2026 matches at Boston Stadium starting June 13.
Commuter Rail round-trip tickets for match days will cost $80, with shuttle buses up to $95.
Match-day parking is priced around $175, and lodging prices have surged in Greater Boston.

title: A first look at the massive outdoor bar coming to Cape Cod this summer
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Backyard by Foley's, Cape Cod's largest outdoor venue, opens in Dennis Port mid-June.
The 33,650 sq. ft. space includes a 5,000 sq. ft. sail-covered bar with seating and activities.
The venue features food trucks, live music, and collaborations with local businesses for events.

title: Chelsea man pleads not guilty after allegedly threatening BU campus, students, deans
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Maximillien Sajous, 29, a BU law student, faces felony charges related to threats against Boston University campus and staff.
Sajous is accused of making a mass shooting threat and posting images of shell casings and a BU dean’s residence.
Sajous is held without bail after three arrest warrants; his internship with the Attorney General's Office was rescinded.

title: Healey refuses to alter RMV policy that denies undercover license plates to ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey upholds RMV policy denying undercover plates to most ICE vehicles, facing Trump administration legal threats.
Assistant Attorney General Brett Shumate demands policy reversal, calling it unconstitutional and a risk to federal agents' safety.
RMV policy restricts confidential plates to agencies involved in criminal, not civil enforcement, limiting issuance to some ICE divisions.

title: Body of Quincy kayaker pulled from Boston Harbor
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tri Trinh, 72, was found dead in Boston Harbor after kayaking from a Quincy beach.
Authorities recovered Trinh's body near Thompson Island; no signs of trauma or foul play were found.
Trinh's body was taken to Beth Israel Deaconess Hospital for an autopsy, results are pending.

title: South Shore school employee pleads guilty to stealing food, equipment for ‘Snack Shack’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Patrick Van Cott, former director of food services for Plymouth Public Schools, pleaded guilty to charges of theft and wire fraud.
Van Cott stole thousands in food and equipment from schools, using USDA and school funds to supply his 'Snack Shack' business.
He faces up to 20 years in prison, a $250,000 fine, and three years of supervised release on wire fraud charges.

title: New Allston hotel wins design award
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Atlas Hotel in Allston won a Lifestyle Hotel Public Space award at the 22nd Annual Hospitality Design Awards.
Located near Harvard's Enterprise Research Campus, The Atlas features design inspired by Ray Oldenburg's 'third places' concept.
The hotel's public space includes a lobby living room with natural materials and art from Boston-affiliated contemporary artists.
